title: Logic, Sets, Counting, and Decision Rules · Decision Notebook: Truth Tables
summary: Audit eligibility rules and resource allocation without double-counting people or contradicting policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg truth tables increment in a tested eligibility and allocation rule set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build truth tables to test compound conditions and policy rules.
Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Truth Tables evidence
steps.instruction: 27 records meet rule A, 20 meet rule B, and 9 meet both. How many meet A or B?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.
